context.description: Allegorical prose section titled “Gewissheit” about a righteous man who sacrifices the present for promised freedom and fulfillment, then doubts the path as the burden grows. A sudden fall reveals a horrific vision of chained sufferers, death, and disease, which he interprets as hell before returning to the burdened road.
normalization_notes: Title inferred from source file name.
Raw category/subcategory were mapped to the closest closed taxonomy fit.

rows.paragraph_text: A righteous man chooses hard work and honesty, hoping for freedom and fulfillment at the end of his road.
rows.text_content: A righteous man chooses hard work and honesty, hoping for freedom and fulfillment at the end of his road.

rows.paragraph_text: He grows weary as sacrifice keeps being demanded, and he begins to doubt the promised destination while others seem certain of the same path.
rows.text_content: He grows weary as sacrifice keeps being demanded, and he begins to doubt the promised destination while others seem certain of the same path.

rows.paragraph_text: After a fall he sees a horrific vision of chained sufferers, corpses, and disease, experiences it as hell, and returns to his burdened path.
rows.text_content: After a fall he sees a horrific vision of chained sufferers, corpses, and disease, experiences it as hell, and returns to his burdened path.
